name: Web Audit
description: Standard for an immutable, actor-attributed history of consequential actions on a domain entity — who did what, when, and before→after. Every state transition, cleared condition, accepted/rejected document, and issued decision is recorded append-only and surfaced as a queryable timeline. It fixes the AuditEvent schema and a swappable audit provider (in-memory, event-sourced, or an OSCAL backend behind one seam), and composes: a lifecycle (Web Lifecycle) transition becomes one audit event automatically. Distinct from Web States change-tracking (reactive, transient mutation observation) and from Web Compliance's enforcement-evidence trail (the platform twin, same schema, different subject): this is the domain object's operational record.
summary: An immutable, actor-attributed timeline of consequential actions — who did what, when, and before→after.
tierEvidence: Frontier UI ships blocks/audit/AuditProvider.ts — a shipped constellation consumer of the append-only audit-history protocol.
